ns_unidir_adv: Configure bidirectional OSPF/ISIS adjacencies. Check interface configurations, ensure both sides have matching network statements, and verify authentication settings match on both ends.
ns_asym_edge_adv: Ensure OSPF/ISIS link costs are symmetric. Review interface cost configurations on both sides of each link. Asymmetric costs can cause routing loops and suboptimal paths.
ns_asym_path_fail: ✗ Potential asymmetric paths detected ({0} asymmetric edges)
ns_asym_path_adv: Review routing policies and ensure consistent path selection. Check for route-maps, prefix-lists, or policy-based routing that might cause different paths in each direction. Use the Analytics -> Asymmetric paths report for details.
ns_conn_adv: All nodes should be reachable. Check for missing links, verify OSPF/ISIS adjacencies are established, and ensure all areas are properly connected through area border routers (ABRs) or level transitions.
ns_backup_none_adv: No terminated networks found in the topology.
ns_backup_pct_adv: Add redundant paths to non-backed up networks. Configure HSRP/VRRP for critical subnets, add backup links, or implement ECMP to provide multiple paths. Currently {0} networks lack backup paths.
ns_spof_small: ✓ Graph too small for SPOF analysis
ns_spof_ok: ✓ No single points of failure detected
ns_spof_adv: Add redundancy for critical nodes: {0}. Configure redundant links, add backup routers, or implement clustering/high-availability for these nodes.
ns_dup_adv: Review network termination: {0}. Ensure each subnet is advertised by only the appropriate router(s). Check for duplicate LSA advertisements or misconfigured network statements.
ns_ecmp_adv: Review ECMP settings. Ensure load balancing is properly configured, check for inconsistent link costs that prevent ECMP, and verify that backup paths via ECMP are available. Use Analytics -> Main and backup paths via ECMP report for details.
ns_area_skip: ⚠ Area design check skipped (IS-IS or disabled)
ns_area_none_adv: Configure at least one OSPF area. Consider using area 0 as backbone and additional areas for network segmentation.
ns_area_single: ✓ Single area design (suitable for small networks)
ns_area_many_adv: Consider consolidating areas. Having more than {0} areas can increase complexity and ABR load. Review area boundaries and merge smaller areas where possible.
ns_crit_small: ✓ Graph too small for redundancy analysis
ns_crit_ok: ✓ Critical nodes have redundancy ({0} critical nodes checked)
ns_crit_adv: Add redundant links to critical nodes: {0}. These nodes are on many shortest paths and their failure would significantly impact the network.
ns_mon_adv: Enable network monitoring by configuring a watcher. Monitoring helps detect topology changes, link failures, and metric changes in real-time.
